items.title: Data Center Firm Switch Seeks $2 Billion in Funding Round
items.description: Switch Inc. is kicking off a private funding round led by Andreessen Horowitz that could raise about $2 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: Gold Extends Gain After Warsh Remarks Ease Rate-Hike Prospects
items.description: Gold extended a rebound after a speech by US Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh dampened speculation the central bank may hike interest rates this year to tackle inflation.

items.title: Korean Stocks Fall as Jitters Over Meta and Apple Hit Chipmakers
items.description: South Korean stocks slumped as Meta Platforms Inc.’s plan to sell computing power raised questions over excess in AI capacity, driving a selloff in chipmakers.

items.title: SEC Probes Alleged Insider Trades That Cost Susquehanna

items.title: Hamilton Lane Targets $220 Million for First China Yuan Fund
items.description: Hamilton Lane Inc., a private market investor that manages $1 trillion, is planning its first yuan-denominated fund to target discounted Chinese assets, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: KKR-Backed Musinsa Eyes 'Fast Growth' in China

items.title: Traders Plot Worst-Case Scenario for Yen If Crisis Hits
items.description: The yen sliding to a once-unthinkable 200 per dollar level is now a medium-term risk — albeit an extreme one — for some investors.

items.title: Oil Extends Drop as More Barrels Flow Through Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Oil fell for a third day as flows through the Strait of Hormuz climbed and there were signs of progress in indirect talks between the US and Iran.

items.title: Global Treasurers Fuel Record $36 Billion of Kangaroo Bond Sales
items.description: Australia’s bond market is drawing increasing interest from funding chiefs worldwide, pushing issuance to a record high, as a flood of Asian capital cap borrowing costs and compete for deals.
